title: Marrubium flavum
intro: Marrubium flavum, also known as Yellow Horehound, is a perennial plant belonging to the Lamiaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Marrubium flavum contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicines like syrups and cough drops to alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Marrubium flavum belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Marrubium, which includes other species of lemongrass. The Lamiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the mint family. Members of this family are often characterized by their square stems and opposite leaves. Marrubium flavum is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its medicinal properties.
